meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging Mastery: Build a Portfolio to Master AI Development
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to build a high-impact portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to master software engineering as a student.
content.answer_block: The inability to debug is often a symptom of building in isolation. By shifting focus to portfolio-driven development, you force yourself to document logic, iterate on failures, and utilize AI agents like Claude Code to audit your stack. Transform your debugging process from a bottleneck into a repeatable engineering workflow.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. Your code is broken, the stack trace is a cryptic labyrinth, and the impostor syndrome is setting in. Every hour spent chasing a silent bug feels like a step backward, leaving you wondering if you have what it takes to ship real-world software.

But here is the industry secret: even the best architects don't 'fix' bugs—they engineer systems that are resilient, observable, and documented. By pivoting your approach to portfolio-driven development, you stop treating errors as failures and start treating them as data points. It is time to stop guessing and start building with the precision of a senior engineer.

steps.title: Architecting for Observability
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, define your system boundaries. This makes debugging trivial because you know exactly where the failure occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and suggest three logging interceptors that will make debugging asynchronous state transitions easier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect-mode 'map dependencies and identify potential failure points'

steps.title: Automated Code Auditing
steps.description: Use AI agents to perform a 'pre-flight' check on your logic before you even attempt to run the compiler, catching edge-case bugs early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a static analysis on this module. Identify race conditions and suggest a more robust error-handling pattern.

steps.title: Documenting the 'Why'
steps.description: A portfolio project is only as good as its documentation. Use your debugging sessions to create technical case studies for your GitHub.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Turn this debug log into a concise 'Post-Mortem' technical report suitable for a software engineering portfolio.

steps.title: Implementing Unit-Test Driven Development
steps.description: Stop manual testing. Build a test suite that acts as your safety net, allowing you to refactor with absolute confidence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for this function that covers 100% of edge cases, including null pointers and unexpected types.

steps.title: Refactoring for Readability
steps.description: Complex code is hard to debug. Simplify your logic so your future self can understand it in seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to be more declarative. Remove nested conditionals and improve variable naming for clarity.

pro_tips: Never debug in the main branch; create a 'hotfix' branch to isolate changes and keep your commit history clean for potential employers.
Use Claude Code to explain what a specific, confusing error message actually means in plain English before attempting a fix.
Treat your error logs as a portfolio asset; a well-documented bug fix demonstrates more seniority than a perfect, bug-free repo.
Configure Antigravity to keep a 'Context Store' of your project state to avoid re-explaining the codebase in every session.
Always run 'diff' on AI-generated fixes to understand exactly what changed; never blindly accept code you cannot explain to a hiring manager.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your project, push your changes to a public repository and write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post to accompany your portfolio entry.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to stop fighting your code and start mastering it. The difference between a student and a senior engineer is the ability to turn a broken build into a feature launch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
